target: State Addenda to Disclosure Document (Exhibit J-1)

overrides: Liquidated damages may be unenforceable under Civil Code §1671
Termination/non-renewal governed by Bus. & Prof. Code §§20000-20043
NY governing law may not be enforceable in CA
VA venue may not be enforceable in CA
General release cannot waive Franchise Investment Law rights
Material modification requires disclosure document approved by Dept. of Corporations
General release cannot relieve franchisor from liability under HI franchise law
7-day disclosure delivery required before signing
IL Franchise Disclosure Act governs termination and non-renewal (815 ILCS 705/19-20)
Governing law/venue/jury waiver cannot waive IL FDA rights
Settlement agreements and general releases allowed but cannot waive Act compliance
MD law may supersede FA on termination/renewal
Bankruptcy termination may not be enforceable under federal law
Franchisee may sue in Maryland for MD FRDL claims (3-year SOL)
General release cannot waive MD FRDL rights
No waiver of claims under state franchise law via questionnaire or acknowledgment
Cannot prohibit franchisee from joining association
Cannot require waiver of statutory rights
Termination only for good cause (30-day cure minimum)
Non-renewal requires fair compensation (if <5yr term and post-term restrictions)
Renewal on generally available terms
No out-of-state arbitration/litigation
Transfer denial only for good cause
Trademark indemnification required
90-day termination notice (60-day cure), 180-day non-renewal notice
Liquidated damages prohibited (MN Rule 2860.4400J)
No out-of-state litigation required
No jury trial waiver
General release cannot relieve MN FIL liability
Franchisee may terminate on any grounds available by law
Franchisor assignment only to willing and able assignee
NY Franchises Law governs NY causes of action
GBL Article 33 §§680-695 may supersede FA
General release on renewal/transfer may not be enforceable
Injunctive relief requires proper proofs and court grant
ALL liquidated damages provisions deleted
ND law supersedes NY law where conflicting
Out-of-state venue provisions deleted
Jury trial waiver deleted
General release cannot waive ND FDA liability
Bankruptcy termination may not be enforceable under federal law
Release on transfer/renewal does not waive RI FIA claims
Out-of-state venue and foreign governing law void for RI FIA claims
Cancellation without reasonable cause is unlawful (VA Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564)
WA FIPA (RCW 19.100.180) may supersede FA on termination/renewal
Release/waiver limited — only via negotiated settlement with independent counsel
WA law prevails in conflicts of law
Transfer fees limited to reasonable estimated or actual costs
Arbitration/mediation in WA or mutually agreed location
Non-compete void unless employee earnings >$100K/yr or IC earnings >$250K/yr
No-poach provisions (franchisor/franchisee employee hiring restrictions) void
